title: Measure real OpenROAD seed variance for terminal sigmoid PWL under Nangate45
hypothesis: Once FLOW_RANDOM_SEED is propagated into detailed placement, global routing, and detailed routing, repeated synthesis with different seeds will expose the achievable timing spread for a fixed terminal sigmoid PWL block configuration.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What critical-path spread appears when the same terminal sigmoid PWL block is synthesized repeatedly with distinct OpenROAD flow seeds?
include: five repeated L1 trials of the same config and sweep with different FLOW_RANDOM_SEED values
follow_on_broad_sweep: larger seed-variance passes on higher-complexity blocks if this first real-seed item shows measurable spread

expected_benefit: establish an empirical timing variance range attributable to real OpenROAD seed changes
risks: the chosen block may still be too simple to show meaningful seed sensitivity

expected_result.reason: capture the critical-path spread across real flow seeds for one fixed implementation
